title: Victoria Tyees

infobox.value: were a [minor league baseball](/wiki/Minor_league_baseball "Minor league baseball") team based in [Victoria, British Columbia](/wiki/Victoria,_British_Columbia "Victoria, British Columbia"). From 1952 to 1954, a team of the same name played in the [Class A](/wiki/Class_A_(baseball) "Class A (baseball)") level [Western International League](/wiki/Western_International_League "Western International League"). In 1954, they were affiliated with the [Portland Beavers](/wiki/Portland_Beavers "Portland Beavers"). They played their home games at [Royal Athletic Park](/wiki/Royal_Athletic_Park "Royal Athletic Park").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Canadian minor league baseball team

The **Victoria Tyees** were a [minor league baseball](/wiki/Minor_league_baseball "Minor league baseball") team based in [Victoria, British Columbia](/wiki/Victoria,_British_Columbia "Victoria, British Columbia"). They originally played in the [Class B](/wiki/Class_B_(baseball) "Class B (baseball)") level [Northwest International League](/wiki/Northwest_International_League "Northwest International League") in 1919. From 1952 to 1954, a team of the same name played in the [Class A](/wiki/Class_A_(baseball) "Class A (baseball)") level [Western International League](/wiki/Western_International_League "Western International League"). In 1954, they were affiliated with the [Portland Beavers](/wiki/Portland_Beavers "Portland Beavers"). They played their home games at [Royal Athletic Park](/wiki/Royal_Athletic_Park "Royal Athletic Park").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Notable players

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Victoria_Tyees&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Notable players")]

* [Jim Clark](/wiki/Jim_Clark_(infielder) "Jim Clark (infielder)"), [Dain Clay](/wiki/Dain_Clay "Dain Clay"), [Cecil Garriott](/wiki/Cecil_Garriott "Cecil Garriott"), [Jay Heard](/wiki/Jay_Heard "Jay Heard"), [Eddie Lake](/wiki/Eddie_Lake "Eddie Lake"), [Steve Mesner](/wiki/Steve_Mesner "Steve Mesner") and [Neill Sheridan](/wiki/Neill_Sheridan "Neill Sheridan") played for the team.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)
